title: Supply
description: Question on the law of supply and the factors that affect producer supply

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following movements would represent a decrease in the quantity supplied, but not associated with a decrease in supply?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate grid with grid lines at each unit plots two upward-sloping lines labeled S 1 and S 2. The horizontal axis is labeled Q and ranges from 0 to 9 in increments of 1. The vertical axis is labeled P and ranges from 0 to 10 in increments of 1. Line S 1 starts at (0, 3) and ends at (9, 7). Line S 2 starts at (0, 5) and ends at (9, 9). Four points are marked: Point A at (2, 6) on S 2, Point B at (2, 4) on S 1, Point C at (6, 8) on S 2, and Point D at (6, 6) on S 1.
options.caption: Two supply curves

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following describes the movement indicated by the arrow in the graph shown?**

[[☃ radio 2]]

options.alt: A coordinate grid plots two upward-sloping straight lines labeled S 1 and S 2. The horizontal axis is labeled Q, and the vertical axis is labeled P. The Q-axis ranges from 0 to 9 in increments of 1, and the P-axis ranges from 0 to 9 in increments of 1. Line S 1 is above and parallel to S 2. A rightward arrow is placed between the lines, indicating a shift from S 1 to S 2. Line S 1 joins (0, 5) and (7, 10). Line S 2 joins (0, 2) and (7, 7)
options.caption: Two supply curves

choices.content: An increase in the quantity supplied
A decrease in the quantity supplied
A movement along a supply curve

assessment_items.question_content:
Use the figure shown to answer the question.

[[☃ image 1]]

**According to each supply curve, what quantities would be produced at $\$30$?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate grid plots two upward-sloping straight lines labeled S 1 and S 2. The horizontal axis is labeled Q, and the vertical axis is labeled P. The Q-axis ranges from 0 to 90 in increments of 10, and the P-axis ranges from 0 to 90 in increments of 10. Line S 2 lies above and parallel to line S 1. Line S 1 joins (0, 10) and (80, 50). Line S 2 joins (0, 30) and (80, 70)
options.caption: Two supply curves

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following movements would be described as an increase in the supply of a good?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate grid with grid lines at each unit plots two upward-sloping lines labeled S 1 and S 2. The horizontal axis is labeled Q and ranges from 0 to 9 in increments of 1. The vertical axis is labeled P and ranges from 0 to 10 in increments of 1. Line S 1 starts at (0, 3) and ends at (8, 7). Line S 2 starts at (0, 5) and ends at (8, 9). Four points are marked: Point A at (2, 6) on S 2, Point B at (2, 4) on S 1, Point C at (6, 8) on S 2, and Point D at (6, 6) on S 1.
options.caption: Two supply curves

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the law of supply?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: As price increases, quantity supplied increases.
Producers sell the same amount of a good no matter its price.
Sellers set the price that demanders pay.

assessment_items.question_content:
**According to the law of supply, what happens when the price of a good increases?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: People are willing to buy what is supplied.
The supply curve shifts to the left.
The supply curve shifts to the right.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following best describes the law of supply?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: As price increases, quantity supplied increases.
Producers sell the same amount of a good no matter its price.
Sellers set the price that demanders pay.

assessment_items.question_content:
**Which of the following statements accurately describes how an event will impact supply?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: An increase in costs of production will cause an increase in the supply curve
An increase in demand will cause an increase in supply
An increase in price will cause supply to increase
A decrease in price will cause supply to increase
A decrease in resource costs will cause an increase in the supply curve

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following could cause the movement indicated by the arrow in the graph shown here?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate grid plots two upward-sloping straight lines labeled S 1 and S 2. The horizontal axis is labeled Q, and the vertical axis is labeled P. The Q-axis ranges from 0 to 90 in increments of 10, and the P-axis ranges from 0 to 90 in increments of 10. Line S 2 lies above and parallel to line S 1. A leftward arrow is placed between the two lines, indicating a shift from S 2 to S 1. Line S 1 joins (0, 20) and (80, 60). Line S 2 joins (0, 40) and (7, 80)
options.caption: Two supply curves

choices.content: Seller's expectation that the good's price will fall in the future

assessment_items.question_content:
[[☃ image 1]]

**Which of the following could cause the shift from S1 to S2 shown in the graph shown?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

options.alt: A coordinate grid plots two upward-sloping straight lines labeled S 1 and S 2. The horizontal axis is labeled Q, and the vertical axis is labeled P. Both axes are unmarked. Line S 1 is above and parallel to line S 2. A rightward arrow is placed between the lines, indicating a shift from S 1 to S 2.
options.caption: Two supply curves

choices.content: An increases in taxes on producers
A reduction in raw material prices
A decrease in the number of producers in the market

assessment_items.question_content:
Land in the United States can be used to produce either soybeans or corn. The price of soybean fertilizer has fallen due to cheaper imports from China.

**What will change for soybeans and corn?**

[[☃ radio 3]]

choices.content: Supply of both corn and soybeans will increase
Supply of soybeans will decrease and supply of corn will increase
Supply of soybeans will increase and the supply of corn will decrease
Quantity supplied of corn and quantity supplied of soybeans will both increase
Quantity supplied of corn and quantity supplied of soybeans will both decrease

assessment_items.question_content:
Assume that basketballs and volleyballs use identical resources to produce and can be made with the same workers and machinery.

**How would an increase in the price of basketballs affect the production of basketballs and volleyballs?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: Quantity supplied of basketballs will not be affected.
Quantity supplied of volleyballs will increase
Quantity supplied of basketballs will decrease

assessment_items.question_content:
Coffee bean merchants notice that coffee prices are at a historic low today, but they expect the price of coffee beans to increase in the next six months.

**How will the expectation of an increase in future prices most likely affect the supply of coffee beans on the market today?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: There will be a movement along the same supply curve to a new higher quantity supplied
There will be a movement along the same supply curve to a new lower quantity supplied
